vulnerabilities.name: fs-concat-user-input-js
vulnerabilities.message: File read with user-controlled path via string concatenation — path traversal risk. An attacker can use "../" sequences to access arbitrary files. Use path.resolve() and verify the resolved path starts with the allowed directory.

vulnerabilities.name: detect-child-process
vulnerabilities.message: Detected calls to child_process from a function argument `req`. This could lead to a command injection if the input is user controllable. Try to avoid calls to child_process, and if it is needed ensure user input is correctly sanitized or sandboxed.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

vulnerabilities.name: res-send-template-literal-js
vulnerabilities.message: Reflected XSS — res.send() outputs a template literal that interpolates user-controlled input. Never build HTML responses by interpolating req.query or req.body values directly. Use a templating engine with auto-escaping (Handlebars, Nunjucks, EJS with <%= escape %>) or sanitize with he/DOMPurify.

vulnerabilities.name: raw-html-format
vulnerabilities.message: User data flows into the host portion of this manually-constructed HTML. This can introduce a Cross-Site-Scripting (XSS) vulnerability if this comes from user-provided input. Consider using a sanitization library such as DOMPurify to sanitize the HTML within.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: direct-response-write
vulnerabilities.message: Detected directly writing to a Response object from user-defined input. This bypasses any HTML escaping and may expose your application to a Cross-Site-scripting (XSS) vulnerability. Instead, use 'resp.render()' to render safely escaped HTML.
vulnerabilities.cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

vulnerabilities.name: hardcoded-api-key-assignment-js-extended
vulnerabilities.message: Hardcoded credential assigned to a sensitive variable name. Use environment variables instead: process.env.VAR_NAME

vulnerabilities.name: hardcoded-jwt-secret
vulnerabilities.message: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).

vulnerabilities.name: express-open-redirect
vulnerabilities.message: res.redirect() with user-controlled URL — open redirect vulnerability. An attacker can redirect victims to phishing sites. Validate the destination against an allowlist of trusted paths/domains or use relative paths only.

vulnerabilities.name: hardcoded-jwt-secret
vulnerabilities.message: A hard-coded credential was detected. It is not recommended to store credentials in source-code, as this risks secrets being leaked and used by either an internal or external malicious adversary. It is recommended to use environment variables to securely provide credentials or retrieve credentials from a secure vault or HSM (Hardware Security Module).
